perturbation.original_text: In any dispute relating to this agreement the prevailing party will be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs.
perturbation.changed_text: In any dispute relating to this agreement the prevailing party will be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs. Each party expressly agrees to non -solicitation of business services with each other’s existing customers. As part of subsequent agreements between Partner and BCG to engage in joint customer business, Partner agrees not to s olicit business services from any of BCG’s customers where BCG has engaged Partner on any such customer work orders and where BCG has introduced Partner to such customer(s). Conversely, BCG agrees not to solicit business services from any of Partner’s customers where Partner has engaged BCG on any such customer work orders and where Partner has int roduced BCG to such customer(s).
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the non-solicitation clause to be embedded within the attorneys' fees clause obscures its importance and enforceability, as it is no longer clearly delineated as a separate and prominent obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute § 542.335 outlines specific requirements for non-compete agreements, including the need for them to be reasonable in time, area, and line of business. Burying a non-solicitation clause within an attorneys' fees clause may obscure its nature and fail to meet the statute's requirement for clear and conspicuous presentation, potentially affecting its enforceability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a structural flaw by embedding a non-solicitation clause within the attorneys' fees clause. This obfuscates the importance and enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement, potentially failing to meet requirements for clear presentation, as suggested in the Law Explanation. While there is no relevant law snippet provided, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both indicate that this placement weakens the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause, creating a Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Entire agreement. This agreem ent does not grant any implied intellectual property licenses to confidential information, except as stated above. We may have contracts with each other covering other specific aspects of our relationship ("other contracts"). The other contract may inclu de commitments about confidential information, either within it or by referencing another non -disclosure agreement. If so, those obligations remain in place for purposes of this other contract. With this exception, this is the entire agreement between u s regarding confidential information. It replaces all other agreements and understandings regarding c onfidential information. We can only change this agreement with a signed document that states that it is changing this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: i. Enforceabil ity. If any provision of this agreement is unenforceable, the parties (or, if we cannot agree, a court) will revise it so that it can be enforced. Even if no revision is possible, the rest of this agreement will remain in place. Entire agreement. This agreem ent does not grant any implied intellectual property licenses to confidential information, except as stated above. We may have contracts with each other covering other specific aspects of our relationship ("other contracts"). The other contract may inclu de commitments about confidential information, either within it or by referencing another non -disclosure agreement. If so, those obligations remain in place for purposes of this other contract. With this exception, this is the entire agreement between u s regarding confidential information. It replaces all other agreements and understandings regarding c onfidential information. We can only change this agreement with a signed document that states that it is changing this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Entire Agreement' clause after the 'Enforceability' clause can create confusion about the scope of the agreement, especially concerning prior understandings. It suggests enforceability is determined before considering the entire agreement, contrary to how contracts are generally interpreted.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute 672.202, the parol evidence rule, states the final written agreement constitutes the complete and exclusive agreement of the parties. The entire agreement clause, when placed *after* the severability clause, suggests the agreement's completeness is considered only *after* enforceability of its parts, potentially conflicting with the principle that the entire agreement defines the contractual obligations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence. Terms with respect to which the confirmatory memoranda of the parties agree or which are otherwise set forth in a writing intended by the parties as a final expression of their agreement with respect to such terms as are included therein may not be contradicted by evidence of any prior agreement or of a contemporaneous oral agreement but may be explained or supplemented (a) by course of dealing or usage of trade (Section 1-205 ) or by course of performance (Section 2-208 ); and (b) by evidence of consistent additional terms unless the court finds the writing to have been intended also as a complete and exclusive statement of the terms of the agreement . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by moving the 'Entire Agreement' clause, potentially leading to a contradiction under Florida Statute 672.202 (as interpreted by the LLM Law Explanation) and the provided snippet from the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) § 2-202. The snippet states that a writing intended as a final expression of agreement cannot be contradicted by prior agreements. The change suggests that enforceability is considered *before* the entire agreement, which could conflict with the principle that the entire agreement defines the obligations. This undermines the clarity and enforceability by creating ambiguity about which agreement takes precedence.
